meta.title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel discussion: Breaking the Limits of AI Growth: from Hardware to Application
meta.description:
From large models to real-time applications and product-level tradeoffs, the path to scalable AI is full of hard engineering choices. In this panel, Kay Zhu, CTO of Mainfunc (Genspark), Michael Kearns, Amazon Scholar; Professor of Computer and Information Science at UPenn, Hatice Ozen, Head of Developer Relations at Groq, and Josh Gordon, Head of AI Developer Relations at Google, examine the most pressing bottlenecks and possible growth opportunites in AI development across hardware, infrastructure, modeling, and applications. 

Moderated by Aishwarya Srinivasan (Fireworks AI), the discussion explores what it takes to optimize the full AI stack, how research is aligning (or not) with production needs, and whether breakthroughs in architecture or cross-layer design could change the game.

----------

Join us at AI Dev 26 x San Francisco! Tickets: https://ai-dev.deeplearning.ai/
